text.title: Living Documentation Toolchain
text.summary: Generator selection criteria, linting tools, and CI pipeline steps for Docs-as-Code.

list.item: MkDocs + Material: Python projects, technical docs, fast builds, Material theme
Docusaurus: Node.js/React projects, developer portals, MDX support
VitePress: Vue projects, very fast builds
Starlight (Astro): modern DX, content collections with Zod schemas

rule.statement: Run `mkdocs build --strict` (or equivalent `--fail-on-warning`) in CI to catch broken references before deploy.
rule.rationale: Non-strict builds silently skip broken includes and missing pages. Strict mode surfaces all issues at build time.

rule.statement: Add three linting steps to the docs CI job: prose lint (Vale), link validation (linkinator), and API spec validation (Spectral for OpenAPI).
rule.rationale: Each tool catches a different failure class: Vale catches style drift, linkinator catches broken URLs, Spectral catches spec regressions. Running all three is the minimum viable doc quality gate.

antipattern.description: Running Vale with default rules on technical writing without customization.
antipattern.reason: Default Vale rules produce too many false positives on technical prose (passive voice warnings, capitalization rules). Configure `.vale.ini` and disable overly aggressive rules before using in CI.

antipattern.description: Agents updating OpenAPI specs without running a breaking-change check.
antipattern.reason: Renamed fields and removed endpoints are breaking changes. Run `redocly diff` or `bump.sh diff` before committing spec changes to surface regressions.

text.reference: GitHub Actions workflow for the complete docs CI pipeline
MkDocs Material configuration with recommended plugins
